rationale: Exploit our moderate military advantage and the enemy’s tactical setback to degrade their forward logistics and prevent consolidation of defensive positions.
Reposition armored reserves to forward staging areas to maintain operational momentum and deter counteroffensives along the central front.
Conduct high-visibility armored exercises near the expanded frontline to signal resolve and test enemy reaction times.
Leverage the moderate crisis level and recent battlefield disruption to degrade Tethyan command-and-control systems and exploit potential lapses in cyber vigilance.
Amplify economic uncertainty by seeding false narratives about supply chain collapse, exploiting declining wheat prices and sanctions pressure.
Target rail infrastructure in occupied zones to delay mobilization of Tethyan reinforcements ahead of anticipated dry-season operations.
With sanctions at 37% and wheat prices falling, lock in discounted bulk purchases of grain and critical inputs to insulate our war economy from future shocks.
Coordinate with allied producers to restrict export of rare minerals used in precision munitions, leveraging strong international support (77%) to tighten economic vise.
Launch a domestic war bond campaign to fund military sustainment, capitalizing on stable domestic morale and redirecting private capital toward defense without inflationary spending.
Given our unfavorable military balance and 13% territory loss, reinforcing defensive positions on the eastern front consolidates our recent tactical gains and denies further enemy advances.
Strategic redeployment of reserves to protect critical supply routes counters Novaris’ overextension while maintaining operational flexibility.
A show of force in the Black Sea deters enemy naval escalation and signals resolve to international partners without provoking full escalation.
With crisis at 6.0 and confirmed strain on enemy logistics, a targeted cyber operation can disrupt command systems and exploit battlefield momentum.
Undermining rail logistics in captured territories degrades Novaris’ ability to sustain frontline forces and capitalizes on their overextension.
Disseminating false troop movement reports sows confusion in enemy planning and enhances the effectiveness of our defensive posture.
With international support at 77%, we must institutionalize solidarity by deepening military-logistical coordination within the coalition to sustain pressure.
Parallel talks create off-ramps if the enemy seeks de-escalation due to economic strain, preserving our diplomatic initiative.
Establishing monitored aid routes bolsters civilian resilience and highlights Novaris’ responsibility for humanitarian costs.
With sanctions at 37% and declining wheat prices destabilizing markets, securing grain and fuel reserves insulates our economy from further shocks.
Issuing domestic war bonds mobilizes citizen investment in defense, offsetting military expenditure pressures amid reserve depletion.
Locking in alternative export routes for agricultural surplus leverages price declines to strengthen economic partnerships and circumvent sanction spillover.
